commands.review_summary:
The PR extends `openclaw secrets audit` to enumerate adjacent config backup filenames, scan them for plaintext config secret targets, and add regression coverage for a stale backup with a provider `apiKey`.

Reproducibility: yes. From source, create an adjacent `openclaw.json.pre-update` or JSON5-formatted `openclaw.json.bak` containing a plaintext secret; the proposed matcher/parser path will not report it.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for security-scope coverage fixes and real behavior proof; automation should not take over while external proof is missing.

target.title: Audit config backups for plaintext secrets
target.body:
## Summary

/claim #11829

This closes a concrete remaining gap from the API-key protection roadmap: stale adjacent `openclaw.json` backups can still contain plaintext credentials after the active config has moved to SecretRefs. `openclaw secrets audit` now scans adjacent config backup files and reports plaintext credential targets found there.

## Changes

- Add `listConfigBackupPaths()` to find adjacent backup names such as `openclaw.json.bak`, `openclaw.json.bak.*`, and `openclaw.json.*.bak`.
- Extend secrets audit to scan those backup files with the same configured secret target registry used for the active config.
- Report plaintext findings with the backup file path and JSON path so users can clean the stale copy.
- Add regression coverage for a SecretRef-clean active config with a stale backup that still contains a provider `apiKey`.

## Validation

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts -t "flags plaintext secrets left in adjacent config backups"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed | 23 skipped (24)
```

Notes:
- `git diff --check` passes.
- A full `pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts` run stayed in the repo transform/startup path for several minutes in this local environment, so I stopped it and kept the validation focused on the new regression.

## Bounty

TaskBounty task: https://www.task-bounty.com/tasks/fix-security-roadmap-protecting-api-keys-from-agen-uej5sq
target.author: lukaivanic
